items.headline: U.S. Stocks Plummet Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. stock markets experienced a significant downturn on Friday, March 13, 2026, with all three major indices closing in negative territory and marking their lowest closes so far this year. Investor sentiment was heavily impacted by intensified geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East and a continued surge in crude oil prices, which disrupted global supply chains. Decliners significantly outnumbered advancers on both the NYSE and Nasdaq, reflecting broad-based selling pressure across the market.

items.headline: Chevron Corp. Gains on Oil Surge
items.summary: Shares of Chevron Corp. advanced as U.S. benchmark West Texas Intermediate (WTI) futures jumped over 9.7% to settle at $95.73 per barrel, and Brent futures climbed 9.2% to settle at $100.46 per barrel.

items.headline: Exxon Mobil Corp. Rises with Crude Prices
items.summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. saw its stock price increase, benefiting from the significant climb in crude oil prices amid geopolitical tensions.

items.headline: JPMorgan Moves Higher in Mixed Market
items.summary: JPMorgan's stock rose, contributing to the gains seen in some financial sector stocks within the otherwise declining S&P 500.

items.headline: Ulta Beauty Leads S&P 500 Declines
items.summary: Ulta Beauty experienced the steepest decline among S&P 500 stocks after its latest quarterly results missed analysts' profit targets, compounded by a substantial increase in administrative expenses.

items.headline: Adobe Falls Despite Earnings Beat
items.summary: Software maker Adobe's shares dropped as investors were reportedly underwhelmed by the company's forecast for its recurring subscription revenue, despite reporting sales and profit figures that beat Wall Street expectations.

items.headline: GIII Apparel Group Plummets on Earnings Miss
items.summary: GIII Apparel Group saw a sharp drop in its stock price after reporting fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings that significantly lagged the Zacks Consensus Estimate.

items.headline: Energy Sector Outperforms Amid Broad Market Declines
items.summary: Amid a challenging market, the Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) was a notable outlier, rising 2.5%. This positive movement was largely attributed to the sustained surge in crude oil prices driven by Middle East geopolitical instability. Conversely, nine out of the eleven S&P 500 sectors ended in negative territory, with the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR (XLP) falling 1.3% and the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR (XLRE) declining 1.2%. The technology sector also experienced weak performance.

items.headline: Q4 2025 GDP Growth Revised Down to 0.7%
items.summary: The second estimate for the fourth quarter of 2025 indicated that real gross domestic product (GDP) increased at a subdued annual rate of 0.7%. This figure represents a downward revision of 0.7 percentage points from the advance estimate, primarily reflecting lower revisions to exports, consumer spending, government spending, and investment. For the entirety of 2025, real GDP grew by 2.1%.

items.headline: Inflation Measures Show Modest Increases
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, the personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index, increased 2.8% year-over-year in January, slightly down from December's 2.9%. Excluding volatile food and energy components, the core PCE price index rose 3.1% year-over-year, marking its largest increase since March 2024. February consumer prices also saw a 0.3% rise, with headline CPI up 2.4% year-over-year.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Poised to Hold Rates; Capital Requirements to Ease
items.summary: Economists have adjusted their expectations for a Federal Reserve interest rate cut, now anticipating it in June rather than March, with a virtual certainty that rates will remain unchanged at the upcoming March 18th meeting. Separately, the Federal Reserve Board announced proposals to revise bank capital requirements in the coming week, which are expected to result in a 'small amount' of decrease for overall capital requirements, particularly larger reductions for smaller banks.

items.headline: Mixed Labor Market Data Released
items.summary: Initial jobless claims decreased by 1,000 to 213,000 for the week ended March 7, slightly above consensus estimates. Continuing claims also saw a decrease, falling by 21,000 to 1.85 million for the week ended February 28. Additionally, U.S. job openings in January jumped to nearly 7 million.
